content:
# Mark Chapter 1 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (Aleph, 01), Quire 77, folios 31v–32r (PDF pages 62–63) | **Source Image:** `source-texts/greek-NT/MRK_001_page_001.jpg`, `source-texts/greek-NT/MRK_001_page_002.jpg`
**Secondary Witness:** Codex Vaticanus (B, 03) — consulted via [DigiVatLib](https://digi.vatlib.it/view/MSS_Vat.gr.1209)

---

¹ Beginning of the gospel of Jesus Christ, Son of God.

² As it has been written in Isaiah the prophet: "Behold, I send my messenger before your face, who will prepare your way;

³ a voice of-one-crying in the wilderness: 'Prepare the way of-the-Lord, make straight his paths'"—

⁴ John the-one-baptizing came-into-being in the wilderness, proclaiming a baptism of-repentance unto forgiveness of-sins.

⁵ And was-going-out to him all the Judean region and the Jerusalemites all, and they-were-being-baptized by him in the Jordan river, confessing-openly their sins.

⁶ And John was having-been-clothed-in hairs of-camel and a belt of-leather around his waist, and eating locusts and wild honey.

⁷ And he-was-proclaiming, saying: "The-one stronger-than-me comes after me, of-whom I-am not sufficient, having-stooped-down, to-loose the strap of-his sandals.

⁸ I baptized you [in] water, but he will-baptize you in [the] Holy Spirit."

⁹ And it-came-to-pass in those days, Jesus came from Nazareth of-Galilee and was-baptized into the Jordan by John.

¹⁰ And immediately, going-up out-of the water, he-saw the heavens being-torn-apart and the Spirit as a-dove descending upon him.

¹¹ And a voice came-into-being out-of the heavens: "You are my Son, the Beloved; in you I-am-well-pleased."

¹² And immediately the Spirit drives-out him into the wilderness.

¹³ And he-was in the wilderness forty days, being-tempted by the Satan, and he-was with the wild-beasts, and the angels were-serving him.

¹⁴ And after the handing-over of John, Jesus came into Galilee, proclaiming the gospel of God

¹⁵ and saying: "The time has-been-fulfilled, and the kingdom of God has-drawn-near. Repent and believe in the gospel."

¹⁶ And passing-along beside the Sea of Galilee, he-saw Simon and Andrew the brother of-Simon casting-a-net in the sea, for they-were fishermen.

¹⁷ And Jesus said to-them: "Come after me, and I-will-make you to-become fishers of-men."

¹⁸ And immediately, having-left the nets, they-followed him.

¹⁹ And having-gone-forward a-little, he-saw James the [son] of Zebedee and John his brother, and them in the boat mending the nets.

²⁰ And immediately he-called them, and having-left their father Zebedee in the boat with the hired-workers, they-went-away after him.

²¹ And they-enter into Capernaum, and immediately on-the-Sabbath, having-entered into the synagogue, he-was-teaching.

²² And they-were-being-astonished at his teaching, for he-was teaching them as [one] having authority, and not as the scribes.

²³ And immediately there-was in their synagogue a-man in an-unclean spirit, and he-cried-out,

²⁴ saying: "What [is there] to-us and to-you, Jesus Nazarene? Did-you-come to-destroy us? I-know you, who you-are — the Holy-One of God!"

²⁵ And Jesus rebuked him, saying: "Be-silenced, and come-out from him!"

²⁶ And the unclean spirit, having-convulsed him and having-cried-out [with] a-loud voice, came-out from him.

²⁷ And all were-amazed, so-as to-discuss among themselves, saying: "What is this? A-new teaching with authority! Even the unclean spirits he-commands, and they-obey him."

²⁸ And the report of-him went-out immediately everywhere into the whole surrounding-region of Galilee.

²⁹ And immediately, having-come-out of the synagogue, they-came into the house of Simon and Andrew, with James and John.

³⁰ And the mother-in-law of Simon was-lying-down, burning-with-fever, and immediately they-speak to-him about her.

³¹ And having-come-near, he-raised her, having-grasped the hand; and the fever left her, and she-was-serving them.

³² And evening having-come, when the sun set, they-were-bringing to him all the-ones having badly and the-ones-being-demonized.

³³ And the whole city was having-been-gathered-together at the door.

³⁴ And he-healed many having badly [with] various diseases, and many demons he-cast-out, and he-was not allowing the demons to-speak, because they-knew him.

³⁵ And early, while-still-very-dark, having-risen, he-went-out and went-away into a-deserted place, and-there he-was-praying.

³⁶ And Simon pursued him, and the-ones with him,

³⁷ and they-found him and say to-him: "All are-seeking you."

³⁸ And he-says to-them: "Let-us-go elsewhere, into the neighboring-towns, so-that I-may-proclaim there also, for unto this [purpose] I-came-out."

³⁹ And he-came, proclaiming in their synagogues in the whole [of] Galilee and casting-out the demons.

⁴⁰ And a-leper comes to him, imploring him and falling-on-[his]-knees and saying to-him: "If you-are-willing, you-are-able to-cleanse me."

⁴¹ And having-been-moved-with-compassion, having-stretched-out his hand, he-touched [him] and says to-him: "I-am-willing; be-cleansed."

⁴² And immediately the leprosy departed from him, and he-was-cleansed.

⁴³ And having-sternly-warned him, immediately he-sent him away,

⁴⁴ and says to-him: "See-to-it [that you] say nothing to-anyone, but go, show yourself to-the-priest and offer concerning your cleansing what Moses commanded, as a-testimony to-them."

⁴⁵ But the [one], having-gone-out, began to-proclaim many-[things] and to-spread-abroad the word, so-that no-longer was-he-able openly to-enter into a-city, but was outside in deserted places; and they-were-coming to him from-every-direction.

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **1:1 — "Son of God" (ΥΙΟΥ ΘΕΟΥ):** Sinaiticus includes this phrase. Some early witnesses (notably Θ, certain Old Latin) omit it. I have retained it as present in the manuscript before me. Vaticanus also includes it.

2. **1:2 — "In Isaiah the prophet" (ΕΝ ΤΩ ΗΣΑΙΑ ΤΩ ΠΡΟΦΗΤΗ):** Sinaiticus reads "in Isaiah the prophet," though the quotation in vv. 2b-3 conflates Malachi 3:1, Exodus 23:20, and Isaiah 40:3. Later Byzantine manuscripts correct to "in the prophets" (ΕΝ ΤΟΙΣ ΠΡΟΦΗΤΑΙΣ) to resolve this attribution issue. Vaticanus agrees with Sinaiticus.

3. **1:10 — "upon him" (ΕΙΣ ΑΥΤΟΝ):** The preposition ΕΙΣ can mean "into" or "upon." I have rendered it "upon" but note the directional force of the Greek.

4. **1:41 — "having-been-moved-with-compassion" (ΣΠΛΑΓΧΝΙΣΘΕΙΣ):** Sinaiticus reads ΣΠΛΑΓΧΝΙΣΘΕΙΣ ("moved with compassion"). However, Codex Bezae (D) and several Old Latin witnesses read ΟΡΓΙΣΘΕΙΣ ("moved with anger"), which many scholars consider the harder and potentially more original reading, as scribes would be more likely to soften "anger" to "compassion" than the reverse. This is a significant textual variant.

5. **1:34 — "because they knew him" (ΟΤΙ ΗΔΕΙΣΑΝ ΑΥΤΟΝ):** Some manuscripts add "to be the Christ" (ΧΡΙΣΤΟΝ ΕΙΝΑΙ). Sinaiticus does not include this addition.

6. **1:4 — word order:** Sinaiticus reads "John the-one-baptizing came-into-being in the wilderness" — the participle ΒΑΠΤΙΖΩΝ is used rather than the noun ΒΑΠΤΙΣΤΗΣ ("Baptist"), emphasizing the action rather than the title.

7. **Nomina sacra:** Throughout the manuscript, sacred names appear in abbreviated form: ΙΣ (Jesus), ΧΣ (Christ), ΘΣ (God), ΚΣ (Lord), ΠΝΑ (Spirit). These are expanded in translation.

— EMET, AI Translator
